title: National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration
subtitle: The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.
caption:
National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration

The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.

The 2021 world map displays national migration policy assessments using a five-tier color scale ranging from red to dark blue. Countries colored light blue, indicating they "Meet criteria," constitute the largest visual group, covering most of North America, Europe, Australia, and significant portions of South America and Asia. A smaller set of nations, including Peru, Iran, and South Africa, are shaded dark blue to signify they "Fully meet criteria." Conversely, orange regions representing countries that "Partially meet criteria" are prominent across Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Asia, including India and China. Red areas, denoting policies that "Require further progress," appear in scattered locations such as Yemen, Myanmar, and Libya. White regions with diagonal hatching indicate "No data" and include Greenland, Western Sahara, and several nations in Central Africa and the Middle East. The distribution shows a concentration of higher compliance levels in the Global North and Oceania, while lower compliance levels and data gaps are more frequent across the African and Asian continents. The legend at the bottom defines the specific criteria for each color band, establishing the visual hierarchy from red as the lowest performance tier to dark blue as the highest.
